textPlain: This seems like a well balanced approach. I do love the abuse mitigation measures in place to dissuade casually malicious actors. The fact that providing evidence itself is a deterrent just goes to show how ill intentioned most of them are.
How does it make sense to ask an app developer to appeal on behalf of a platform they have zero control over?

It doesn't, but platforms basically do everything they can to claim the various common-carrier liability shields in DMCA-like laws.  In the U.S. that means they forward the takedown request to whomever generated the content, and in theory should allow that generator to comply, or publish a counterclaim.The whole system falls on the floor though when the common carriers aren't, and have low quality processes that don't actually enable the counterclaim half of this process.
